data.title: Wihlborgs Fastigheter to Buy Carbon Reduction Certificates from Sweden's Öresundskraft
data.description:
Swedish real estate company Wihlborgs Fastigheter agreed to buy carbon reduction certificates from Swedish municipal energy company Öresundskraft.

The 10-year agreement is expected to commence in 2028 when Öresundskraft's carbon dioxide separation facility at the Filbornaverket plant in Sweden becomes operational. The certificates correspond to 10,000 tonnes of annual emissions.

data.title: Novo Nordisk's head of commercial strategy steps down
data.description:
Novo Nordisk's head of commercial strategy and corporate affairs, Camilla Sylvest, has decided to leave the company, the Danish drugmaker said on Thursday.

Head of Novo's rare disease unit, Ludovic Helfgott, will immediately assume the responsibility for product and portfolio strategy, including commercial strategy, medical affairs and business development across all therapy areas, Novo Nordisk said in a statement.

data.title: Euro Hits 25-week High
data.description:
EURUSD increased to a 25-week high of 1.10.

Over the past 4 weeks, Euro US Dollar gained 3.08%, and in the last 12 months, it increased 1.1%.

data.title: German carmakers slide after Trump administration confirms car tariffs
data.description:
** Shares in German carmakers slip in early Frankfurt trade after Trump administration confirmed 25% global car and truck tariffs to take effect on Thursday, while duties on auto parts on May 3

** Shares in Volkswagen , Porsche SE , Porsche AG , BMW , Mercedes are down between 2.7% and 3.6%

** Shares in German car parts maker Continental , truck makers Daimler Truck and Traton slide between 2.4% and 3.5%

** Barclays sees a "long-term reality" of 25% EU-US auto tariffs as the base case now, leading to higher costs, capital spend to modify footprints, price inflation to consumers, and likely lower volumes

** "OEMs now face the tough choice about how much of the additional tariffs they should absorb themselves vs attempting to pass them on to US customers and dealers," Barclays says

** Although the announcement was expected by the market, it is a net negative for the earnings momentum of the original equipment manufacturers (OEM), J.P.Morgan says

** "Assuming a 10% blanket tariff, we think the potential impact of the tariff is manageable assuming Truck OEMs could pass on single digit price increase in the market," the broker adds

data.title: UK electricals retailer Currys raises profit outlook after robust trading
data.description:
British electricals retailer Currys on Thursday raised its full-year profit forecast, saying trading since early January had been robust.

The group, which sells consumer electricals from computers to washing machines and TVs, said adjusted profit before tax in its 2024/25 year was now expected to be around 160 million pounds ($209 million), up from previous guidance of 145 million-155 million pounds.

($1 = 0.7638 pounds)
